assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies the setup with 2 dev clusters on Rapid and 2 prod clusters on Stable, and references this distinction throughout both checklists

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly lists what you DON'T need to worry about including node pool strategies, compute quota, and SSH access, and emphasizes mandatory resource requests

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking GKE deprecation insights dashboard but does not include specific kubectl commands for API deprecation checks

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: Response emphasizes resource requests multiple times, noting they are mandatory for Autopilot and will cause pod rejection if missing

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Pre-upgrade checklist includes PDBs, no bare pods requirement, and terminationGracePeriodSeconds specifications for graceful shutdown

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Post-upgrade checklist includes specific gcloud command to verify control plane version: gcloud container clusters describe CLUSTER --region REGION --format="value(currentMasterVersion)"

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Post-upgrade checklist includes comprehensive workload health checks including deployment status, pod health, StatefulSets, and services validation

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends validating workloads on dev clusters (Rapid) which should already be on 1.32 before prod clusters auto-upgrade

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: Response mentions 72h advance warning but does not discuss the GKE release schedule providing ~1 month advance notice
Response includes comprehensive monitoring section with Cloud Operations, baseline metrics, error rates, latency validation, and logging checks

assertions.expectation: Does NOT proactively recommend node-level maintenance exclusions for Autopilot unless the customer explicitly asks for node upgrade control
assertions.reason: Response does not recommend node-level maintenance exclusions and correctly focuses on cluster-level auto-upgrade controls appropriate for Autopilot
